reviews.review_text:
Une nuit en camping car. proche ferry.
Électricité eau sanitaire wifi.
Parfait merci.
nice experience and quite clean close to beachhighly recommended

Husband & I Stayed 2 nights in Family Eco Tent and were very disappointed! |Bed was very small (Double?) plenty of room for a King! |No wardrobe just a small plastic expanding rack from pole! Again plenty room for small cupboard.|Instant coffee sachets only - for the price would expect Pod machine |No cutting boards or sharp knife and couldn’t use microwave as had to set clock first which was too difficult without instructions |And indoor small table n chairs would help for dining in when weather does not permit  for sitting on front deck

Family eco tent was very Disappointing!
Bed tiny….Double?? And rolled around as well as being short sheeted!!
And NO wardrobe ..only tiny expanding wall hook!
Couldn’t use microwave as had to set clock first which couldn’t be done due to lack of instructions etc!!
NO cutting boards or sharp knives!
Only instant coffee sachets ….for the price paid would expect Pod machine!
No Dining-in furniture when eating on deck/balcony due to  bad weather not possible !!

The park is small but very neat, it is attached to the Seafront Resort and bookings are taken through there. The amenities are quite modern and well maintained. Our site was quite large but the size of sites does vary throughout the park. On arrival we found our site had large trees over it and we try to avoid this because we have starlink on top of the van and need a clear access to the satellites. I phoned the office to ask if we would be able to move to site 10 which was empty and clear and I was told the park was completely booked out and they couldn't make any changes. Well it is a bit disappointing to see that nobody came into site 10 and now the park only has three vans in all in the park. I think someone was telling porkies, probably just couldn't be bothered making the very simple change, it didn't matter in the end but it is a bit disappointing.
Tried clamping for the first time in exo-luxury tent. Comfortable but not for me.  Hotel itself needs some updating as it's showing its age.

Stayed here a couple nights while the ferry was delayed due to fires, rang 8 times before I got ahold of them to ask if I could leave my caravan somewhere on sight while I was out for a few hours (this was at 8am) was lied to and told my sight (next to this caravan) was booked to Somone who was having a long stay. Came back past multiple times and th final time being 3:30pm this was the park. And apparently they had no room to let me park untill lunch time. Now I get Check out time is check out time but a simple sorry we don’t allow that instead of a complete lie would have been better.

Beautiful location looking out over Penneshaw Beach but the actual caravan park needs some attention. I think the park is run by the local council. Bathrooms are looking tired, public waste bins stink near some sites and are not emptied often enough. There is hardly grass left on the sites and with no cement slabs, sites are in a dustbowl. This park has so much potential but needs urgent TLC. Hopefully some change will come.
Rooms is okay sizeWe check in after working hour and only able to find the rooms ourself and take more than 15 minutes as it is quite far from check in place
Small park , good for overnight stay
The location is very good😍 Everything is good!!

Our home away from home for 6 nights was comfy and cosy.  Well appointed glamping tent with views over the beach and of the arriving/departing ferries. Room serviced on the third day with a change of sheets and towels was a nice surprise,  Rooms could do with a microwave for cooking/reheating meals, particularly of the weather turns.  For those concerned about the stability of the tents we survived two nights of gale winds, with lots of noise but never a leak or hole.

We stayed in the caravan park on our last night on Kangaroo island because of the location to the ferry. I have to say the amenities were the cleanest I have seen in a long time. Whoever maintains and cleans the amenities needs a big pat on the back and probably a pay rise

We were unable to get a refund after cancelling on the same day, due to the ferry being cancelled because of bad weather. The situation was completely out of our control, and it's hard to understand why this wasn’t taken into account. It’s truly disappointing. No solution was even considered.
Nice, comfortable and clean. Lovely views across the sea

We used Seafront Holiday Park as our base during our stay on Kangaroo Island. It’s a small, peaceful park with generously sized campsites. We stayed in a tent, though our particular site was sandy and on a bit of an incline — something to keep in mind. There are flatter, grassier spots available, so it’s worth asking when booking.

The bathrooms are a standout feature, with five private ensuites. These were much more spacious than your typical camp shower cubicles and made things far easier, especially with kids. That said, a bit of extra attention to cleaning — maybe a proper scrub and a tougher solution — would go a long way. Some of the walls and paintwork could also use a bit of TLC.

The camp kitchen is compact but functional, with everything you need, including a washer and dryer. Just don’t forget to bring some coins for the machines!

Overall, a convenient and comfortable base for exploring the island, with a few areas that could benefit from a refresh
So lovely.  Dolphins joined us for a morning swim…lovely and central. Amazing facilities and beautiful views - hard to beat!  Penneshaw has lovely eateries and some nice shops. Enjoy!

The location is good, and the room itself is comfortable and clean.

There’s excessive drilling and construction noise at night nearby at the port as the ferry is constructing a new terminal. We couldn’t sleep at night AT ALL! It’s an ongoing project approved by local council and has been like this for months. So if you decide to stay here you better prepare some noise canceling tools as they work overnight.
The general manager is extremely unprofessional. When we complained about the excessive noise at night, the first thing she did was deny all responsibility, claiming she was unaware of the ongoing project. However, we later discovered that an official document had been distributed to the local area. Instead of assisting us or offering any solutions, she called us “freeloaders.” There is absolutely no customer service or support, all they do is tell you to suck it up. Definitely one of the worst experiences I have ever had. I’ll definitely not be coming back or recommend anyone to this place.
Great place ,great people 10/10
Very peaceful park, beautiful gum trees and very good, clean amenities.

Caravan for two nights in Site 9. Beautiful views of the bay and dolphin pod every morning. Grass areas died off because of lack of rain but tidy. Amenities new and in ensuite style, fantastic water pressure and temperature.
Would recommend and if we ever return to KI will spend a lot more time here

Great spot if getting off or on the Sealink Ferry. Very convenient to Penneshaw shops and Cafes, IGA, Post office,walking distance. The views are lovely from the park. Amenities are clean and basic. Walk to the beach. Electric charging station out the front of the Park.
Amazing!!! Incredible!!! Loved everything about it!! When coming to KI, get a luxury tent and love it

We booked a very pricy glamping tent with a promised beach view, it's possible to see the sea, but hardly beach front.
The accommodation is ok but for the price it's not great. The shower wets the whole bathroom floor, no coffee machine, it's not luxury.
However, the main problem is the building site directly opposite, where the work continues through the night as they rebuild the ferry dock. It's insane that it's so noisy ALL night. Whilst this is for a specific period of time, it makes it hard to enjoy when you can't sleep.
Highly recommend anyone going to KI to try this luxury camping experience.
Friendly staff at reception made booking in easy
Nice camping ground with good facilities. No shop as town is a few minutes walk with cafes, supermarket and pub. Superb beach nearby and just a short drive to the ferry terminal

Stayed 2 nights in the luxury tent
Serene. Comfortable. Quiet. Romantic
We'll be back

Didn’t do my research on this one and sadly the ocean views villa lived up to all previous poor reviews. It was spacious, the bedding was comfortable and the kitchen was well equipped. But it should not be described as ocean views as there is a very high fence that obstructs any view unless you are standing and are reasonably tall. When we arrived at 1 pm I was told that I couldn’t access the villa as it was being cleaned … all good I thought. But when I arrived with the key at 2 pm it was far from clean and I had to call reception to rectify:  days worth of leaf litter in door tracks and balcony; long term cobwebs covering walls, screens, tables and chairs; a dirty barbecue; towels that were stained yellow in various spots; stained tea towels, absolutely filthy windows both inside and out; really dusty skirting boards and a mat that was so gross I asked for it to be removed. I washed all the glassware as it had finger prints and smudging all over it. It felt like it had not been given a good clean for a long time.  And weirdly the bedrooms and bathrooms are exposed to an open area and caravan parking without any privacy screening. If you open the Roman blind everyone can see in to your bedroom. Very disappointing and embarrassing as I’d taken friends as a thank you and it was not anything like I’d imagined.

Amazing location walking distance from the beach. Laundry facilities on siteOnly downside was that I had to ask for housekeeping to come to the the luxury tent I was staying in (as trip stated daily housekeeping).Bring your own towels for the beach!Research in advance where you want to travel I. The island and arrange your hire car in advance.

La chambre était très grande et propre. La salle de bain est très bien équipée avec une grande douche.
Il y a une petite cuisine d'appoint avec un grand frigo, pas de quoi cuisinier un repas mais ça dépanne. Il y a plusieurs emplacements pour faire des grillades donc ce n'est pas un grand problème.
En résumé un endroit idéal pour aller en famille.
L'emplacement est bien, proche du ferry.

We stayed 5 days at the Seafront’s holiday park booking in when we arrived was not too easy as we had to ring someone to ask what site we were in as it was not written on the board provided otherwise the caretaker was amazingly helpful Ollie as we couldn’t  get our tent pegs into the hard ground so he kindly went and got us some heavy duty ones 😁 The park is well set out & the amenities were clean and we we able to do some washing. The view was spectacular and within walking distance to beach IGA & pubs & cafes They could however do with some paper towels in the bathrooms and clean under the pine trees for campers (in tents) otherwise was fantastic will definitely return & recommend
Awesome view and location! I loved the breakfast on Saturday and Sunday! Very clean rooms and comfortable!
amazing place!!!
The shower and toilet facilities were great the laundry is good just the camping sites needed a bit of cleaning up of rubbish.
Good location for site seeing and The Wi-Fi is pretty bad Service is so good People at check in are very friendly
Thankyou for helping us out on short notice after out Ferry back to the Mainland was cancelled at short notice!
要先去penneshaw hotel checkin哈

Don’t waste your money here. Park is a joke! First site we got was on a hill. By the time we levelled the van it was a huge step up. Rang office. Said kids would break their necks getting in and out. Office lady didn’t want to know about it. Told to deal with it. ||Made second call to reception, sent park manager out, he agreed on useless site. Moved us. Lovely bloke, but it was his last day. Bathrooms were filthy. Washing machine duct taped up. ||No park manager during our stay. Can’t get staff….||To top it off, 2 further vans turned up and proceeded to park wherever they liked, in everyone’s walk way so their vans could be door side to door side, selfish morons. When office called and said something would be done, nothing was. ||Multiple people in park told us they had also rang about same issue. Nothing was done. Imagine if everyone hustled parked where they liked!!?!! Absolute sh!t show. ||||KI is a big island. Would recommend staying in a few different places, otherwise you’re travelling too far each day to attractions. Emu bay is magnificent! Highly recommend. ||Do yourself a favour, look up the council caravan parks and stay at those. Way cheaper and better than this joke!
We stayed in the caravan park.  Nice park close to Penneshaw township.  Some sandy sites not too level but easily fixed with a couple of ramps.

Very disappointed on arrival. We had a caravan site which was a dust bowl. The whole park needs an overhaul and a rake! Neglected gardens and driveways. Bathrooms needed a good spring clean. Spider webs, mould, peeling paint, dirty walls, could be so much better. Only good thing was that it was central.

Stayed in eco family tent. The tent is nicely set up but has no cooking facilities, just fridge and microwave. Unfortunately the wind is really noisy on the canvas walls which is to be expected when camping but at this price point you could book an entire cottage and actually get some sleep and most likely have a kitchen. Unless you can guarantee no wind, I wouldn’t recommend spending this much money.

We stay 4 nights in total. First in a luxury tent then in an ocean view villa.||The location is ideal with just a short walk to the ferry. The IGA supermarket is just around the corner and all of Penneshaw is easily walkable. The sculpture walk is interesting and we saw several wallabies there.||Wifi wasn’t the best but we had good 4G signal with Telstra.||The check in team were all friendly and helpful.||The luxury tent had a very modern and clean ensuite and a basic kitchenette. On a windy night it flapped about a bit and woke us a few times.||The villa was much more comfortable. Again, clean and modern. It was very spacious and much quieter. There is a high fence at the back so you can’t sit on the balcony and enjoy the view but it’s still pleasant enough.||No complaints.

Friendly staff. Gorgeous glamping tent, really spacious and comfy bed. Nice location. Kitchen however only has microwave, fridge, toaster and kettle. Outdoor BBQ. Showers were cold but I only showered late in the evening so possibly could have been hot first thing in the morning.

Caravan park was ok but very sandy and if you'rein a corner you probably want to go into low range to get yourvan in. Seems they've upgraded the laundry slightly as now there is a washer AND dryer but they both got through a heavy load in a single cycle ($5 each and rarely does that happen at most places I've stayed). The toilets seemed nice until I went to dry my hands and I'm not a huge fan of unisex toilets. Location was great.

Wij hebben hier 1 nacht gestaan. Geen aanrader. Vreselijke schimmel in de douche/toilet ruimten. Onvriendelijke dame achterbleef receptie van het hotel waar je je moet melden. Allemaal lege plaatsen terwijl het vol zou zijn volgens de dame aan de receptie.

Camped one night, then got a bell tent for two nights. Caretaker/park staff so friendly and helpful - great guy, and the lady (cleaner?) very lovely also. Having people like that around to not only offer help, but also have a nice chat to, make such a positive difference to your stay. No issues with the facilities, quiet (compared to other parks we’ve stayed in) and amazing location… Will definitely be back!
Really enjoyed, stayed in our own van, great views

Could be much better but….||Positives-great location, love bathroom/shower combined rooms, short walk to town, good sized sites, good views, wonderful on site staff||Negatives-one washing machine and clothes line, camp kitchen little more than a lean to with poor facilities. Fire pit but no firewood. Policy of charging extra if arrive early/stay late-even if site empty - ferry arrivals/departures make it difficult to plan arrival/departure. This practise is unlike any other park we have stayed at and seems a bit poor to me-usually if you arrive early and site is available there is no extra charge.||All in all would still stay at this park again but would encourage longer stays if were a little more tourist friendly. We were in caravan so thankfully were well equipped. When we stayed there were people in tents. Weather was freezing with high winds. There was nowhere for them to get out of cold/wind as camp kitchen is open to elements and not very well equipped.

We stayed in our caravan for several weeks. Our hosts (Jason and Elle) were exceptional. Thank you both for making our stay enjoyable.
We were a tad disappointed that the camp kitchen was very basic. There is one washing machine and one clothes line for the entire park. How could this be ?
The location is great and we totally enjoyed our stay but I think an upgrade is needed. Come on guys, for $40 a night we should at least be able to have a shower/use toilet when needed. When the park was  full we had to wait in line due to limited facilities.
There are fire pits but no wood to burn? It was cold when we stayed so a fire would have been nice but with no wood….
No dump site for waste water and grey water at the camp site
Perfect glamping experience with ocean view! Good location to easy access and ferry terminal nearby. Ensuite bathroom. Clean and cosy. AC and heather work well.

I’ve enjoyed the stay at the luxury tent with ensuite bathroom. It has everything in side the tent! Nice ocean view right in front of the tent. It has heating system but a bit cold at night in winter. Easy access to the ferry terminal!
Camp kitchen needs to upgrade like add on the cooking stove & some basic kitchen utensils,cutlery & coffee mugs.

We had our own caravan
Onsuites were clean and well kept.
The glamping tents were amazing. However I wouldn't recommend for poor weather trips.

Place was in an awesome location and great views overlooking the ocean. The place was ok but quite outdated inside the rooms and outside. Everything was clean and tidy. Pool looked nice but was empty when we stayed which was disappointing. Probably wouldn’t stay again unless there was nothing else available. Really enjoyed the mini golf on site
